DetectionRule.Name: Malicious PowerShell Execution
DetectionRule.Description: Detects malicious use of PowerShell for execution, including encoded commands, download cradles, and obfuscation techniques commonly used by attackers to bypass security controls.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1059.001 - Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT28, FIN7, Lazarus, Ransomware Groups

RemediationSteps.Step: Block the PowerShell process and isolate the device
Analyze the decoded command to understand the attack
Check for additional malicious activity or persistence
Reset credentials for the compromised account
Enable PowerShell Constrained Language Mode or Script Block Logging

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude approved automation scripts with proper signing
Time-based: Focus on non-business hours execution
User-based: Require approval for high-risk PowerShell usage

DetectionRule.Name: WMI Malicious Execution
DetectionRule.Description: Detects malicious use of Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) for execution, lateral movement, and persistence. Identifies unusual WMI process creation, remote execution, and command execution via WMI.

RemediationSteps.Step: Investigate WMI activity and identify executed commands
Check for lateral movement via WMI to other systems
Review WMI event subscriptions for persistence mechanisms
Reset credentials for accounts used in WMI execution
Restrict WMI access through firewall rules and permissions
Enable WMI activity logging and monitoring

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Time-based: Focus on off-hours WMI activity

DetectionRule.Name: CertUtil for File Download
DetectionRule.Description: Detects abuse of certutil.exe for downloading files, which is commonly used by attackers to download tools, payloads, or exfiltrate data while bypassing security controls that may block other download methods.
